clusters.label: Conference Context
clusters.description: Concepts naming the conference, host institutions, logistics, and community norms around the event itself.

clusters.label: AT Protocol Core
clusters.description: Foundational AT Protocol concepts covering the ecosystem, app protocol, records, sync, and the main architectural split between data and apps.

clusters.label: Protocol Standards
clusters.description: Standards bodies, working groups, and formal protocol machinery that shape interoperability and future evolution.

clusters.label: Identity & DIDs
clusters.description: Identifiers, handles, PLC, DIDs, verification, recovery, and cross-account identity binding across the ecosystem.

clusters.label: Social Graph
clusters.description: Following, followers, lists, starter packs, graph visualization, onboarding, and migration of social connections between services.

clusters.label: Feeds & Curation
clusters.description: Custom feeds, ranking, finite scrolling, priority rules, discovery surfaces, and human or probabilistic curation strategies.

clusters.label: Moderation & Safety
clusters.description: Content moderation, labels, blocks, reports, labelers, community notes, and trust-and-safety operations and tooling.

clusters.label: Privacy & Security
clusters.description: Cryptography, anonymity, secure channels, attestation, privacy-by-design, and protections against surveillance or impersonation.

clusters.label: Developer Tooling
clusters.description: Libraries, SDKs, APIs, runtime helpers, MCP servers, and code-generation tools that help builders integrate with the protocol.

clusters.label: Schemas & Lexicons
clusters.description: Schema languages, lexicons, validation, versioning, translation, and compatibility strategies across changing data models.

clusters.label: Records & Events
clusters.description: The underlying record model of AT Protocol, including commit events, updates, deletions, and durable references between records.

clusters.label: Sync, Relays & PDS
clusters.description: The network layer that moves data between personal data stores, relays, app views, and mirrors across the ecosystem.

clusters.label: Decentralization
clusters.description: Philosophies and mechanisms for federation, permissionlessness, portability, exit, and resistance to lock-in or central control.

clusters.label: Governance & Policy
clusters.description: Corporate governance, law, regulation, public policy, and institutional decision-making around platforms and infrastructure.

clusters.label: Social Organization
clusters.description: Community structure, roles, relationships, mutual aid, norms, stewardship, and the social work of coordination and belonging.

clusters.label: Research & Academia
clusters.description: Academic workflows, scholarly communication, research methods, open science, and the migration of researchers and institutions to new social infrastructure.

clusters.label: Science Data
clusters.description: Scientific datasets, repositories, instruments, metadata, and data-discovery infrastructure used to move research outputs onto the protocol.

clusters.label: Media & Journalism
clusters.description: Newsrooms, media institutions, journalism economics, audience development, and the changing distribution of reporting on platforms.

clusters.label: Creator Economy
clusters.description: Creator monetization, subscriptions, sponsorships, patronage, loyal fans, and business models that support independent publishing.

clusters.label: Music
clusters.description: Music production, rights, metadata, scrobbling, listening communities, and creator-owned music distribution models.

clusters.label: Video & Short Form
clusters.description: Short-form and long-form video, live streaming, watch modes, archival playback, and video-specific platform design.

clusters.label: Images, Annotations & Provenance
clusters.description: Image matching, perceptual hashing, claims, timestamping, OCR, and other mechanisms for annotating media with evidence and history.

clusters.label: Web UI & UX
clusters.description: Interface design, browser behavior, design systems, cues, microcopy, and user experience patterns for web and mobile clients.

clusters.label: Localization & I18n
clusters.description: Translation workflows, locale handling, language-specific UI, and the practical problems of making software usable across languages and regions.

clusters.label: DevOps & Hosting
clusters.description: Cloud, servers, proxies, databases, backups, deployment, caching, and the practical operations of running resilient services.

clusters.label: Developer Workflows
clusters.description: Version control, pull requests, stacked changes, linting, builds, CI/CD, package management, and the mechanics of shipping software safely.

clusters.label: AI & Agents
clusters.description: Language models, agent harnesses, memory, tool use, automation, and the ways AI is being integrated into social and research workflows.

clusters.label: Retrieval & Search
clusters.description: Indexing, retrieval-augmented generation, embeddings, clustering, search APIs, and structured ways of finding relevant information.

clusters.label: Knowledge Graphs
clusters.description: Graph-based representations of claims, evidence, discourse, and linked knowledge used for research and browsing.

clusters.label: Journalism Discovery
clusters.description: Story curation, audience growth, and the design of news and commentary feeds that make journalism easier to find and support.

clusters.label: Community Identity
clusters.description: Identity performance, pronouns, badges, artist identity, queer and trans identity, and the politics of naming oneself in public.

clusters.label: Open Science
clusters.description: Open scientific infrastructure, modular publishing, repositories, preprints, and the push to make research more reusable and public.

clusters.label: Geography & Places
clusters.description: Cities, regions, jurisdictions, territories, and local geographies referenced as places of work, hosting, or community identity.

clusters.label: Ecology & Metaphors
clusters.description: Natural-world metaphors, ecological systems, and philosophical images used to explain resilience, community, and design.

clusters.label: Media History
clusters.description: Historical references about media systems, platform transitions, browser history, and the evolution of networked publishing.

clusters.label: Apps & Products
clusters.description: Specific applications, services, and commercial or community projects built on AT Protocol or referenced as exemplars.

clusters.label: Public Space & Culture
clusters.description: Shared social rituals, community atmosphere, memes, humor, and cultural practices that give online spaces a lived-in feel.

clusters.label: Policy & Law
clusters.description: Legal regimes, regulation, liability, privacy law, platform rules, and public-sector governance shaping networked systems.

clusters.label: Business Models
clusters.description: Market structures, funding strategies, economic incentives, and monetization models for social and research infrastructure.
conceptRkeys: one tool to rule them all

clusters.label: Public Safety
clusters.description: Fire service software, emergency response data, dispatch, inspections, and the operational systems that support public safety work.

clusters.label: Data Modeling
clusters.description: General principles for representing, transforming, and preserving meaning in heterogeneous data across domains.
conceptRkeys: one tool to rule them all

clusters.label: Translations & L10n
clusters.description: Language, translation, and localization as social and technical work, including non-English communities and multilingual interface design.

clusters.label: Apps & Clients
clusters.description: Named clients, forks, and app experiences across the ecosystem, especially those that present protocol data in distinctive ways.

clusters.label: Community Projects
clusters.description: Volunteer-run, community-owned, or local commons projects that steward feeds, calendars, moderation, and civic or cultural infrastructure.
